themes.exampleQuote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
there were a lot of good options for drinks! both drinks my sister and I tried were delicious.
I just wish this place was bigger, gets packed quickly!
the workers are so nice every time I visit!
The wi-fi isn't good enough to work from here so I wouldn't come back for that.

notable_quote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers keep circling back to a handful of standout drinks, the strawberry matcha and tiramisu latte in particular, while the consistent friction points are capacity and wifi reliability rather than anything about the coffee itself.
quarter_narrative: Sixteen Pittsburgh creators posted 17 TikToks featuring Delanie's Coffee Shadyside this quarter, generating 66,299 plays without a single frame coming from the business itself. That ratio is the story of Spring 2026 here: a cafe with a genuinely photogenic product and a cult following among local coffee TikTokers, sitting on the sideline of its own conversation. Against that backdrop, the family leader this issue is Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange, a business that has converted social momentum into a commanding rank. Delanie's has the creator fit to close that gap faster than most; the question is whether it acts on it before the next quarterly cycle.
tldr_read: Sixteen creators filming, zero TikTok presence from the business itself, and an Instagram that posted three times in thirty days. Ones to Watch, #4 in Pittsburgh Coffee Shops.
tldr_meaning: The Creator Fit signal is already near the top of the family, but without the business feeding that momentum on its own channels, the review volume stays thin and the visual catalog stays below the family median.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Ones to Watch, Fourth in Coffee. Sixteen Creators Are Doing the Talking.

playbook.headline: Claim space in the TikTok moment
playbook.action: Open a TikTok account and post one video per week reacting to or resharing the 17 creator clips already in circulation.

playbook.headline: Thin review volume needs reinforcing
playbook.action: With only 104 Google reviews at 82% five-star, a simple in-store QR card at checkout could meaningfully move the review count closer to the family median.

playbook.headline: Fill the visual catalog gaps
playbook.action: Google Photos sits at 96 images across six active categories; adding dedicated interior and food-close-up shots would push the visual catalog toward the family median of 77.
